tagline: A temporal arbitrage mechanism where one man's loyalty rewrites gang economics — and every rewrite costs someone their future.
visualizationReason: Tokyo Revengers is fundamentally a temporal feedback loop: each time leap changes the causal graph, creating new winners and losers in the gang hierarchy. Only a timeline renderer captures how intervention points cascade.

headerFlavor.loreQuote: I'm gonna save you, Mikey. No matter how many times I have to jump.
headerFlavor.sysWarning: // [SYS_OP]: TIME LEAP PROTOCOL ACTIVE — PARADOXICAL INTERVENTION IN PROGRESS

hero.microHook: I can travel through time — but only by watching people die.
hero.thesis: Takemichi's time leaps are a loyalty cascade: each intervention redistributes gang power toward Mikey, but the cost is always someone's future.

powerSystem.name: Time Leap
powerSystem.subtitle: Emotional-Trigger Temporal Jump
powerSystem.loreDesc: Takemichi can travel 12 years into the past by gripping someone's hand at the moment of their death — emotional contact required.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A point-intervention temporal system: can only jump to specific emotional anchor points, not arbitrary times.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Takemichi's first time leap, gripping Hina as she dies.

powerSystem.name: Mikey's Invincibility
powerSystem.subtitle: Dark Impulse Suppression
powerSystem.loreDesc: Mikey is the most powerful fighter in Tokyo, but his "dark impulse" makes him a ticking time bomb.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A latent-failure node: Mikey's capability ceiling is offset by a system-collapse risk that activates under specific triggers.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Mikey single-handedly defeating all of Toman's enemies.

rankings.topTierLore: The supreme capability tier in Tokyo Revengers.
rankings.topTierSystem: Maximum capability across all measured dimensions.

tiers.name: A-Tier
tiers.loreDesc: Exceptional capability holders with system-shaping influence.

tiers.name: B-Tier
tiers.loreDesc: Competent operators with meaningful impact potential.

factions.name: Tokyo Revengers Universe
factions.loreDesc: The primary world of the story.

rules.name: Time Leap Requires Emotional Connection
rules.loreDesc: Takemichi can only leap by experiencing someone's future death through physical contact — he can't leap arbitrarily.
rules.systemDesc: A constraint on temporal intervention: emotional proximity limits control.

rules.name: Gang Loyalty Is the Currency
rules.loreDesc: Each gang member's loyalty creates network stability — betrayal cascades destabilize the entire system.
rules.systemDesc: A loyalty-weighted graph: gang strength is the sum of member commitment edges.

counterplay.mechanic: Time Leap dominates standard opponents through point intervention.
counterplay.loreDesc: Time Leap proves superior against standard threats.
counterplay.systemDesc: A point-intervention temporal system: can only jump to specific emotional anchor points, not arbitrary times.

causalEvents.name: Story Begins
causalEvents.trigger: The inciting incident that sets the narrative in motion.
causalEvents.consequence: Protagonist is forced to engage with the system.
causalEvents.loreDesc: The initial condition that creates the narrative premise.

anomalies.name: Unique Capability
anomalies.loreDesc: The protagonist possesses a capability that transcends normal system rules.
anomalies.systemDesc: A system anomaly: Takemichi's time leaps are a loyalty cascade: each...
anomalies.impact: Creates asymmetric advantage against normal opponents.

systemQuestions.question: What makes Tokyo Revengers's system unique?
systemQuestions.answer: A temporal arbitrage mechanism where one man's loyalty rewrites gang economics — and every rewrite costs someone their future.
